{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01BftRK43jVHTHhpTuMmHyZp","type":"tool_result","content":"{\"content\":\"1: async function waitForEvent(eventName, test, target, timeoutMs = 500) {\\n2:   return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\\n3:     const timeoutCallback = test.step_timeout(() => {\\n4:       reject(`No ${eventName} event received for target ${target}`);\\n5:     }, timeoutMs);\\n6:     target.addEventListener(eventName, (evt) => {\\n7:       clearTimeout(timeoutCallback);\\n8:       resolve(evt);\\n9:     }, { once: true });\\n10:   });\\n11: }\\n12: \\n13: async function waitForScrollendEvent(test, target, timeoutMs = 500) {\\n14:   return waitForEvent(\\\"scrollend\\\", test, target, timeoutMs);\\n15: }\\n16: \\n17: async function waitForScrollendEventNoTimeout(target) {\\n18:   return new Promise((resolve) => {\\n19:     target.addEventListener(\\\"scrollend\\\", resolve);\\n20:   });\\n21: }\\n22: \\n23: // Waits until a rAF callback with no \\\"scroll\\\" event in the last 200ms.\\n24: function waitForDelayWithoutScrollEvent(eventTarget) {\\n25:   const TIMEOUT_IN_MS = 200;\\n26: \\n27:   return new Promise(resolve => {\\n28:     let lastScrollEventTime = performance.now();\\n29: \\n30:     const scrollListener = () => {\\n31:       lastScrollEventTime = performance.now();\\n32:     };\\n33:     eventTarget.addEventListener('scroll', scrollListener);\\n34: \\n35:     const tick = () => {\\n36:       if (performance.now() - lastScrollEventTime > TIMEOUT_IN_MS) {\\n37:         eventTarget.removeEventListener('scroll', scrollListener);\\n38:         resolve();\\n39:         return;\\n40:       }\\n41:       requestAnimationFrame(tick); // wait another frame\\n42:     }\\n43:     requestAnimationFrame(tick);\\n44:   });\\n45: }\\n46: \\n47: // Waits for the end of scrolling. Uses the \\\"scrollend\\\" event if available.\\n48: // Otherwise, fall backs to waitForDelayWithoutScrollEvent().\\n49: function waitForScrollEndFallbackToDelayWithoutScrollEvent(eventTargets) {\\n50:   return new Promise(resolve => {\\n51:     if (!Array.isArray(eventTargets)) {\\n52:       eventTargets = [eventTargets];\\n53:     }\\n54:     let listeners = [];\\n55:     const cleanup = () => {\\n56:       for (const [eventTarget, eventName, listener] of listeners) {\\n57:         eventTarget.removeEventListener(eventName, listener);\\n58:       }\\n59:       listeners = [];\\n60:     }\\n61:     const addListener = (eventTarget, eventName, listener) => {\\n62:       listeners.push([eventTarget, eventName, listener]);\\n63:       eventTarget.addEventListener(eventName, listener);\\n64:     }\\n65:     if (window.onscrollend !== undefined) {\\n66:       // If scrollend is supported, wait for the first scrollend event.\\n67:       for (const eventTarget of eventTargets) {\\n68:         addListener(eventTarget, 'scrollend', () => {\\n69:           cleanup();\\n70:           resolve(eventTarget);\\n71:         });\\n72:       }\\n73:     } else {\\n74:       // Otherwise, wait for the first scroll event, then wait until that\\n75:       // scroller finishes scrolling.\\n76:       for (const eventTarget of eventTargets) {\\n77:         addListener(eventTarget, 'scroll', async () => {\\n78:           cleanup();\\n79:           await waitForDelayWithoutScrollEvent(eventTarget);\\n80:           resolve(eventTarget);\\n81:         });\\n82:       }\\n83:     }\\n84:   });\\n85: }\\n86: \\n87: // Waits for the end of scrolling, but resolves after the given timeout if no\\n88: // scroll event occurs.\\n89: function waitForScrollEndOrTimeout(eventTarget, timeout) {\\n90:   const rafTimeout = new Promise(resolve => {\\n91:     const startTime = performance.now();\\n92:     const tick = () => {\\n93:       if (performance.now() - startTime >= timeout) {\\n94:         resolve();\\n95:       } else {\\n96:         requestAnimationFrame(tick);\\n97:       }\\n98:     };\\n99:     requestAnimationFrame(tick);\\n100:   });\\n101: \\n102:   return Promise.race([\\n103:     waitForScrollEndFallbackToDelayWithoutScrollEvent(eventTarget),\\n104:     rafTimeout\\n105:   ]);\\n106: }\\n107: \\n108: async function waitForPointercancelEvent(test, target, timeoutMs = 500) {\\n109:   return waitForEvent(\\\"pointercancel\\\", test, target, timeoutMs);\\n110: }\\n111: \\n112: // Resets the scroll position to (0,0).  If a scroll is required, then the\\n113: // promise is not resolved until the scrollend event is received.\\n114: async function waitForScrollReset(test, scroller, x = 0, y = 0) {\\n115:   return new Promise(resolve => {\\n116:     if (scroller.scrollLeft == x && scroller.scrollTop == y) {\\n117:       resolve();\\n118:     } else {\\n119:       const eventTarget =\\n120:         scroller == document.scrollingElement ? document : scroller;\\n121:       scroller.scrollTo(x, y);\\n122:       waitForScrollendEventNoTimeout(eventTarget).then(resolve);\\n123:     }\\n124:   });\\n125: }\\n126: \\n127: async function createScrollendPromiseForTarget(test,\\n128:                                                target_div,\\n129:                                                timeoutMs = 500,\\n130:                                                targetIsRoot = false) {\\n131:   return waitForScrollendEvent(test, target_div, timeoutMs).then(evt => {\\n132: Track pending scrollend targets (similar to pending scroll events)\n2. Track the last scroll target(s) and their positions\n3. Core scroll event logic\n- `/workspace/components/script/dom/document_event_handler.rs` - Entry point for scroll events\n- `/workspace/components/script/dom/window.rs` - Window-level scroll handling\n- `/workspace/components/script/script_thread.rs` - Rendering update orchestration\n\n#### 2. **Scroll Event Queueing Mechanism**\n\n**Data Structure (document.rs:583):**\n```rust\npending_scroll_event_targets: DomRefCell<Vec<Dom<EventTarget>>>\n```\n\nThis is a deduplicating queue that:\n- Stores both Document and Element targets that need to receive scroll events\n- Uses `has_pending_scroll_events()` to check if updates needed\n- Automatically deduplicates targets (lines 1757-1761 and 1787-1791) to prevent duplicate events\n\n**Queueing Functions:**\n\n1. **`handle_viewport_scroll_event()` (lines 1747-1771)**\n   - Called when the document/viewport scrolls\n   - Adds Document target to pending queue\n   - Spec: https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#scrolling-events\n\n2. **`handle_element_scroll_event()` (lines 1776-1801)**\n   - Called when individual elements scroll\n   - Adds Element target to pending queue\n\n#### 3. **Scroll Event Firing Process**\n\n**`run_the_scroll_steps()` (lines 1714-1742):**\n\n```rust\npub(crate) fn run_the_scroll_steps(&self, can_gc: CanGc) {\n    // Step 1: TODO(#7673) dispatch pending scrollsnapchanging events\n    \n    // Step 2-3: Fire scroll events for all pending targets\n    rooted_vec!(let notify_list <- self.pending_scroll_event_targets.take().into_iter());\n    for target in notify_list.iter() {\n        if target.downcast::<Document>().is_some() {\n            // Bubbling scroll event\n            target.fire_bubbling_event(Atom::from(\"scroll\"), can_gc);\n        } else if target.downcast::<Element>().is_some() {\n            // Non-bubbling scroll event\n            target.fire_event(Atom::from(\"scroll\"), can_gc);\n        }\n    }\n    \n    // Step 4: TODO(#7673) dispatch pending scrollsnapchange events\n}\n```\n\n**Called from:** script_thread.rs:1202 as part of \"update the rendering\" (step 9 of the HTML event loop)\n\n#### 4. **Scroll Triggering Points**\n\nScroll events are triggered in two places:\n\n1. **From embedder (user interactions):**\n   - `document_event_handler.rs:1664` - `handle_embedder_scroll_event()`\n   - Called when user scrolls with mouse/touch/keyboard\n\n2. **From layout/reflow:**\n   - `window.rs:2514-2519` - Triggered when `UpdatedScrollNodeOffset` reflow phase completes\n   - Checks if scroll position actually changed before queuing event\n\n### Current Implementation Status\n\n**Already Implemented:**\n- ✅ Event handler attribute: `onscrollend` registered in eventtarget.rs:137\n- ✅ IDL bindings: Defined in webidls (EventHandler.webidl, VisualViewport.webidl)\n- ✅ Event handler macro: Line 656 of macros.rs: `event_handler!(scrollend, GetOnscrollend, SetOnscrollend);`\n- ✅ WPT tests exist (30+ test files for scrollend behavior)\n\n**NOT Implemented:**\n- ❌ Actual firing of scrollend events\n- ❌ No pending_scrollend_event_targets queue\n- ❌ No logic to dispatch the actual event\n\n### Recommended Implementation Approach\n\n#### **Phase 1: Create Infrastructure (Minimal Changes)**\n\n1. **Add pending scrollend targets queue to Document struct (document.rs:~583):**\n```rust\npending_scrollend_event_targets: DomRefCell<Vec<Dom<EventTarget>>>,\n```\n\n2. **Add helper method to check for pending scrollend (document.rs:~3963):**\n```rust\nfn has_pending_scrollend_events(&self) -> bool {\n    !self.pending_scrollend_event_targets.borrow().is_empty()\n}\n```\n\n#### **Phase 2: Queue Scrollend Events**\n\nModify `handle_viewport_scroll_event()` and `handle_element_scroll_event()` to queue scrollend targets:\n\n```rust\npub(crate) fn handle_viewport_scroll_event(&self) {\n    // ... existing scroll event queueing code ...\n    \n    // Queue scrollend event (same deduplication logic)\n    let target = self.upcast::<EventTarget>();\n    if !self.pending_scrollend_event_targets.borrow().iter().any(|t| *t == target) {\n        self.pending_scrollend_event_targets.borrow_mut().push(Dom::from_ref(target));\n    }\n}\n```\n\n#### **Phase 3: Fire Scrollend Events**\n\nCreate new method `run_the_scrollend_steps()` or extend `run_the_scroll_steps()`:\n\n**According to CSSOM View spec, scrollend should fire AFTER scroll events complete.** This means:\n\n1. Fire scroll events (existing code)\n2. Then fire scrollend events (new code)\n\n```rust\n// In run_the_scroll_steps(), after firing scroll events:\nrooted_vec!(let scrollend_list <- self.pending_scrollend_event_targets.take().into_iter());\nfor target in scrollend_list.iter() {\n    if target.downcast::<Document>().is_some() {\n        target.fire_bubbling_event(Atom::from(\"scrollend\"), can_gc);\n    } else if target.downcast::<Element>().is_some() {\n        target.fire_event(Atom::from(\"scrollend\"), can_gc);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n#### **Phase 4: Debouncing/Coalescing Logic (Optional, Spec-Required)**\n\nAccording to the CSSOM View specification (https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#scrollend-event), scrollend should only fire once per scroll operation. Current implementation already handles this through:\n\n- Event deduplication (targets only appear once in queue)\n- Queue is cleared after processing in each \"update the rendering\" cycle\n- Multiple scroll events in one cycle don't create multiple scrollend events\n\n#### **Phase 5: Handle Scroll Completion Detection**\n\nThe key challenge: detecting when scrolling has truly completed. Based on the code comment at window.rs:2510-2513:\n\n```rust\n// > If the scroll position did not change as a result of the user interaction or programmatic\n// > invocation, where no translations were applied as a result, then no scrollend event fires\n// > because no scrolling occurred.\n```\n\nThis is already handled by:\n- Only queueing events when `UpdatedScrollNodeOffset` reflow phase runs\n- Which only happens when actual scroll position changes\n\n### Timing Considerations\n\n1. **Current Flow:**\n   - Scroll detected → `handle_*_scroll_event()` called → target queued\n   - Next \"update the rendering\" cycle → `run_the_scroll_steps()` fires all scroll events\n\n2. **With Scrollend:**\n   - Same queue mechanism for scrollend targets\n   - Fire scrollend AFTER scroll events in same rendering cycle\n   - If no more scroll events queued in next cycle, scrollend completes\n\n3. **Smooth Scroll Animations:**\n   - Currently not implemented (TODO #7673)\n   - Scrollend should fire after animation completes, not on first frame\n   - May need coordination with animation frame logic\n\n### Test Coverage\n\nThe WPT tests expect:\n- Scrollend fires after scroll completes\n- Both Document and Element scroll events trigger scrollend\n- Window's scrollend for viewport scrolls\n- Element's scrollend for element scrolls\n- VisualViewport scrollend for viewport panning\n- Multiple scroll events in one cycle = single scrollend\n- No scrollend if scroll position didn't change\n\nAll of these are naturally handled by the proposed implementation.\n\n### Files That Need Changes\n\n1. **components/script/dom/document.rs** (~650 lines of changes)\n   - Add pending_scrollend_event_targets field\n   - Add has_pending_scrollend_events() method\n   - Modify handle_viewport_scroll_event() and handle_element_scroll_event()\n   - Extend run_the_scroll_steps() to fire scrollend events\n\n2. **components/script/dom/eventtarget.rs** (already done - line 137)\n   - \"onscrollend\" already in list\n\n3. **components/script/dom/macros.rs** (already done - line 656)\n   - event_handler macro already included\n\n### Estimated Complexity\n\n**Low to Medium** - Checks if scroll position actually changed before queuing event\n\n### Current Implementation Status\n\n**Already Implemented:**\n- ✅ Event handler attribute: `onscrollend` registered in eventtarget.rs:137\n- ✅ IDL bindings: Defined in webidls (EventHandler.webidl, VisualViewport.webidl)\n- ✅ Event handler macro: Line 656 of macros.rs: `event_handler!(scrollend, GetOnscrollend, SetOnscrollend);`\n- ✅ WPT tests exist (30+ test files for scrollend behavior)\n\n**NOT Implemented:**\n- ❌ Actual firing of scrollend events\n- ❌ No pending_scrollend_event_targets queue\n- ❌ No logic to dispatch the actual event\n\n### Recommended Implementation Approach\n\n#### **Phase 1: Create Infrastructure (Minimal Changes)**\n\n1. **Add pending scrollend targets queue to Document struct (document.rs:~583):**\n```rust\npending_scrollend_event_targets: DomRefCell<Vec<Dom<EventTarget>>>,\n```\n\n2. **Add helper method to check for pending scrollend (document.rs:~3963):**\n```rust\nfn has_pending_scrollend_events(&self) -> bool {\n    !self.pending_scrollend_event_targets.borrow().is_empty()\n}\n```\n\n#### **Phase 2: Queue Scrollend Events**\n\nModify `handle_viewport_scroll_event()` and `handle_element_scroll_event()` to queue scrollend targets:\n\n```rust\npub(crate) fn handle_viewport_scroll_event(&self) {\n    // ... existing scroll event queueing code ...\n    \n    // Queue scrollend event (same deduplication logic)\n    let target = self.upcast::<EventTarget>();\n    if !self.pending_scrollend_event_targets.borrow().iter().any(|t| *t == target) {\n        self.pending_scrollend_event_targets.borrow_mut().push(Dom::from_ref(target));\n    }\n}\n```\n\n#### **Phase 3: Fire Scrollend Events**\n\nCreate new method `run_the_scrollend_steps()` or extend `run_the_scroll_steps()`:\n\n**According to CSSOM View spec, scrollend should fire AFTER scroll events complete.** This means:\n\n1. Fire scroll events (existing code)\n2. Then fire scrollend events (new code)\n\n```rust\n// In run_the_scroll_steps(), after firing scroll events:\nrooted_vec!(let scrollend_list <- self.pending_scrollend_event_targets.take().into_iter());\nfor target in scrollend_list.iter() {\n    if target.downcast::<Document>().is_some() {\n        target.fire_bubbling_event(Atom::from(\"scrollend\"), can_gc);\n    } else if target.downcast::<Element>().is_some() {\n        target.fire_event(Atom::from(\"scrollend\"), can_gc);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n#### **Phase 4: Debouncing/Coalescing Logic (Optional, Spec-Required)**\n\nAccording to the CSSOM View specification (https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#scrollend-event), scrollend should only fire once per scroll operation. Current implementation already handles this through:\n\n- Event deduplication (targets only appear once in queue)\n- Queue is cleared after processing in each \"update the rendering\" cycle\n- Multiple scroll events in one cycle don't create multiple scrollend events\n\n#### **Phase 5: Handle Scroll Completion Detection**\n\nThe key challenge: detecting when scrolling has truly completed. Based on the code comment at window.rs:2510-2513:\n\n```rust\n// > If the scroll position did not change as a result of the user interaction or programmatic\n// > invocation, where no translations were applied as a result, then no scrollend event fires\n// > because no scrolling occurred.\n```\n\nThis is already handled by:\n- Only queueing events when `UpdatedScrollNodeOffset` reflow phase runs\n- Which only happens when actual scroll position changes\n\n### Timing Considerations\n\n1. **Current Flow:**\n   - Scroll detected → `handle_*_scroll_event()` called → target queued\n   - Next \"update the rendering\" cycle → `run_the_scroll_steps()` fires all scroll events\n\n2. **With Scrollend:**\n   - Same queue mechanism for scrollend targets\n   - Fire scrollend AFTER scroll events in same rendering cycle\n   - If no more scroll events queued in next cycle, scrollend completes\n\n3. **Smooth Scroll Animations:**\n   - Currently not implemented (TODO #7673)\n   - Scrollend should fire after animation completes, not on first frame\n   - May need coordination with animation frame logic\n\n### Test Coverage\n\nThe WPT tests expect:\n- Scrollend fires after scroll completes\n- Both Document and Element scroll events trigger scrollend\n- Window's scrollend for viewport scrolls\n- Element's scrollend for element scrolls\n- VisualViewport scrollend for viewport panning\n- Multiple scroll events in one cycle = single scrollend\n- No scrollend if scroll position didn't change\n\nAll of these are naturally handled by the proposed implementation.\n\n### Files That Need Changes\n\n1. **components/script/dom/document.rs** (~650 lines of changes)\n   - Add pending_scrollend_event_targets field\n   - Add has_pending_scrollend_events() method\n   - Modify handle_viewport_scroll_event() and handle_element_scroll_event()\n   - Extend run_the_scroll_steps() to fire scrollend events\n\n2. **components/script/dom/eventtarget.rs** (already done - line 137)\n   - \"onscrollend\" already in list\n\n3. **components/script/dom/macros.rs** (already done - line 656)\n   - event_handler macro already included\n\n### Estimated Complexity\n\n**Low to Medium** - The infrastructure is already 90% there:\n- Event queueing pattern proven with scroll events\n- Deduplication logic reusable\n- Event firing mechanism already implemented\n- IDL/WebIDL already defined\n- Main work is copy-paste of scroll event logic + proper ordering\n\nTotal implementation: ~150-200 lines of code changes."}],"totalDurationMs":79930,"totalTokens":66274,"totalToolUseCount":45,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":860,"cache_read_input_tokens":62952,"output_tokens":2461,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":860,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}}}

#[derive(JSTraceable, MallocSizeOf)]\n230: pub(crate) struct RefreshRedirectDue {\n231:     #[no_trace]\n232:     pub(crate) url: ServoUrl,\n233:     #[ignore_malloc_size_of = \"non-owning\"]\n234: invoke(self, can_gc: CanGc) {\n239:         // After the refresh has come due (as defined below),\n240:         // if the user has not canceled the redirect and, if meta is given,\n241:         // document's active sandboxing flag set does not have the sandboxed\n242:         // automatic features browsing context flag set,\n243:         // then navigate document's node navigable to urlRecord using document,\n244:         // with historyHandling set to \"replace\".\n245:         //\n246:         // TODO: check sandbox\n247:         // TODO: Check if meta was given\n248:         let .load_url(NavigationHistoryBehavior::Replace, false, load_data, can_gc);\n253:     }\n254: }\n255: \n256: #[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, JSTraceable, MallocSizeOf, PartialEq)]\n257: pub(crate) enum MallocSizeOf)]\n287: pub(crate) struct RenderingUpdateReason(u8);\n288: \n289: bitflags! {\n290:     impl RenderingUpdateReason: u8 {\n291:         /// When a `ResizeObserver` starts observing a target, this becomes true, which in turn is a\n292:         /// signal to the [`ScriptThread`] that a rendering update should happen.\n293:         const ResizeObserverStartedObservingTarget = 1 << 0;\n294:         /// When an `IntersectionObserver` starts observing a target, this becomes true, which in turn is a\n295:         /// signal to the [`ScriptThread`] that a rendering update should unloading_cleanup_steps(&self) {\n631:         // Step 1. Let window be document's relevant global object.\n632:         // Step 2. For each WebSocket object webSocket whose relevant global object is window, make disappear webSocket.\n633:         if self.close_outstanding_websockets() {\n634:             // If this affected any WebSocket objects, then make document unsalvageable given document and \"websocket\".\n635:             self.salvageable.set(false);\n636:         }\n637: \n638:         // Step 3. For each WebTransport object transport whose relevant global object is window, run the context cleanup steps given transport.\n639:         // TODO\n640: \n641:         // Step 4. For each EventSource object eventSource whose relevant global object is equal to window, forcibly close eventSource.\n646:             global_scope.close_event_sources();\n647: \n648:             // Step 4.2. debug_assert!(*node.owner_doc() == *self);\n673:         if !node.is_connected() {\n674:             return;\n675:         }\n676: \n677:         let parent = match node.parent_in_flat_tree() {\n678:             Some(parent) => parent,\n679:             None => {\n680:                 // There is no parent so this is the Document node, so we\n681:                 // behave as if we were called with          .set_flag(NodeFlags::HAS_DIRTY_DESCENDANTS, true);\n740:                 self.dirty_root.set(Some(element));\n741:                 return;\n742:             },\n743:         };\n744: \n745: ancestor\");\n759: \n760:         let mut has_dirty_descendants = true;\n761:         for ancestor in dirty_root\n762:             .upcast::<Node>()\n763:             .inclusive_ancestors_in_flat_tree()\n764:         {\n765:             ancestor.set_flag(NodeFlags::HAS_DIRTY_DESCENDANTS, has_dirty_descendants);\n766:             has_dirty_descendants &= *ancestor    listener: &T,\n1119:     ) {\n1120:         let mut map = self.form_id_listener_map.borrow_mut();\n1121:         if let Occupied(mut entry) = map.entry(Atom::from(id)) {\n1122: find_a_potential_indicated_element(&self, fragment: &str) -> Option<DomRoot<Element>> {\n1133:         // Step 1. If there is an element in the document tree whose root is\n1134:         // document and that has an ID equal to fragment, then return the first such element in tree order.\n1135:         // Step 3. Return null.\n1136:         self.get_element_by_id(&Atom::from(fragment))\n1137:             // Step 2. If there is an a element in the document tree whose root is\n1138:             // document that has a name attribute whose value is equal to fragment,\n1139:             // then return the first such element in tree order.\n1140:             .or_else(|| self.get_anchor_by_name(fragment))\n1141:     }\n1142: \n1143:     /// Attempt to find a named element in this page's document.\n1144:     /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#the-indicated-part-of-the-document>\n1145:     fn select_indicated_part(&self, fragment: &str) -> Option<DomRoot<Node>> {\n1146:         // Step 1. If document's URL does not equal url with exclude fragments set to true, then return null.\n1147:         //\n1148:         // Already handled by calling function\n1149: \n1150:         // Step 2. Let fragment be url's fragment.\n1151:         //\n1152:         // Already handled by calling function\n1153: \n1154:         // Step 3. If fragment is the empty string, then return the special value top of the document.\n1155:         if fragment.is_empty() {\n1156:             return Some(DomRoot::from_ref(self.upcast()));\n1157:         }\n1158:         // Step 4. Let potentialIndicatedElement be the result of finding a potential indicated element given document and fragment.\n1159:         if let Some(potential_indicated_element) = self.find_a_potential_indicated_element(fragment)\n1160:         {\n1161:             // Step 5. If potentialIndicatedElement is not null, then return potentialIndicatedElement.\n1162:             return Some(DomRoot::upcast(potential_indicated_element));\n1163:         }\n1164:         // Step 6. Let fragmentBytes be the result of percent-decoding fragment.\n1165:         let fragment_bytes = percent_decode(fragment.as_bytes());\n1166:         // Step 7. Let decodedFragment be the result of running UTF-8 decode without BOM on fragmentBytes.\n1167:         let Ok(decoded_fragment) = fragment_bytes.decode_utf8() else {\n1168:             return None;\n1169:         };\n1170:         // Step 8. Set potentialIndicatedElement to the result of finding a potential indicated element given document and decodedFragment.\n1171:         if let Some(potential_indicated_element) =\n1172:             self.find_a_potential_indicated_element(&decoded_fragment)\n1173:         {\n1174:             // Step 9. If potentialIndicatedElement is not null, then return potentialIndicatedElement.\n1175:             return Some(DomRoot::upcast(potential_indicated_element));\n1176:         }\n1177:         // Step 10. If document's indicated part is null, then set document's target element to null.\n1188:         //\n1189:         // > For an HTML document document, its indicated part is the result of\n1190:         // > selecting the indicated part given document and document's URL.\n1191:         let Some(indicated_part) = self.select_indicated_part(fragment) else {\n1192:             self.set_target_element(None);\n1193:             return;\n1194:         };\n1195:         // Step 2. \n1328:     /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#focus-fixup-rule>\n1329:     pub(crate) fn perform_focus_fixup_rule(&self, not_focusable: &Element, can_gc: CanGc) {\n1330:         // Return      }\n1335: \n1336:         let implicit_transaction = self.focus_transaction.borrow().is_none();\n1337: \n1338:         if implicit_transaction {\n1339:   focus_initiator,\n1365:             FocusOptions {\n1366:                 preventScroll: true,\n1367:             },\n1368:             can_gc,\n1369:         );\n1370:     }\n1371: \n1372:     /// transaction, this method automatically starts and\n1376:     /// commits an implicit transaction.\n1377:     pub(crate) fn request_focus_with_options(\n1378:         &self,\n1379:         elem: Option<&Element>,\n1380:         focus_initiator: FocusInitiator,\n1381:         focus_options: FocusOptions,\n1382:         can_gc: CanGc,\n1383:     ) {\n1384:         // If an element is specified, accordingly after being notified that the\n1410:     /// document's container is removed from the top-level browsing context's\n1411:     /// focus chain (not considering system focus).\n1412:     pub(crate) fn handle_container_unfocus(&self, can_gc: CanGc) {\n1413:         if self.window().parent_info().is_none() {\n1414:             warn!(\"Top-level document cannot be unfocused\");\n1415:             return;\n1416:         }\n1417: \n1418:         // Since this method is called from an event loop, there mustn't be\n1419:         // an in-progress focus transaction\n1420:         assert!(\n1421:             self.focus_transaction.borrow().is_none(),\n1422:             \"there mustn't be an in-progress focus transaction at this point\"\n1423:         );\n1424: \n1425:         // Start an implicit focus transaction\n1426:         self.begin_focus_transaction();\n1427: \n1428:         // Update the transaction\n1429:         {\n1430:             let mut focus_transaction =       self.commit_focus_transaction(FocusInitiator::Remote, can_gc);\n1436:     }\n1437: \n1438:     /// Reassign the focus context to the element that last requested focus during this\n1439:     /// transaction, or the document if no elements requested it.\n1440:     pub(crate) fn commit_focus_transaction(&self, focus_initiator: FocusInitiator, can_gc: CanGc) {\n1441:         let (mut new_focused, new_focus_state, prevent_scroll) = {\n1442:             let focus_transaction = self.focus_transaction.borrow();\n1443:             let focus_transaction = focus_transaction\n1444:                 .as_ref()\n1445:                 .expect(\"no focus transaction in progress\");\n1446:             (\n1447:                 focus_transaction\n1448:                     .element\n1449:                     .as_ref()\n1450:                     .map(|e| DomRoot::from_ref(&**e)),\n1451:                 focus_transaction.has_focus,\n1452:                 focus_transaction.focus_options.preventScroll,\n1453:             )\n1454:         };\n1455:         *self.focus_transaction.borrow_mut() = None;\n1456: \n1457:         if !new_focus_state {\n1458:             // In many browsers, a document forgets its focused area when the\n1459:             // document is removed from the top-level BC's focus chain\n1460:             if new_focused.take().is_some() {\n1461:                 trace!(\n1462:                     \"Forgetting the document's focused area because the \\\n1463:                     document's container was removed from the top-level BC's \\\n1464:                     focus chain\"\n1465:                 );\n1466:             }\n1467:         }\n1468: \n1469:         let old_focused = self.focused.get();\n1470:         let old_focus_state = self.has_focus.get();\n1471: \n1472:         debug!(\n1473:             \"Committing focus transaction: {:?} → {:?}\",\n1474:             (&old_focused, old_focus_state),\n1475:             (&new_focused, new_focus_state),\n1476:         );\n1477: \n1478:         // `*_focused_filtered` indicates the local element (if any) included in\n1479:         // the top-level BC's focus chain.\n1480:         let old_focused_filtered = old_focused.as_ref().filter(|_| old_focus_state);\n1481:         let new_focused_filtered = new_focused.as_ref().filter(|_| new_focus_state);\n1482: \n1483:         let trace_focus_chain = |name, element, doc| {\n1484:             trace!(\n1485:                 \"{} local focus chain: {}\",\n1486:                 name,\n1487:                 match (element, doc) {\n1488:                     (Some(e), _) => format!(\"[{:?}, document]\", e),\n1489:                     (None, true) => \"[document]\".to_owned(),\n1490:                     (None, false) => \"[]\".to_owned(),\n1491:                 }\n1492:             );\n1493:         };\n1494: \n1495:         trace_focus_chain(\"Old\", old_focused_filtered, old_focus_state);\n1496:         trace_focus_chain(\"New\", new_focused_filtered, new_focus_state);\n1497: \n1498:         if old_focused_filtered != new_focused_filtered {\n1499:             if let Some(elem) = &old_focused_filtered {\n1500:                 let node = elem.upcast::<Node>();\n1501:                 elem.set_focus_state(false);\n1502:                 // FIXME: pass appropriate relatedTarget\n1503:                 if node.is_connected() {\n1504:                     self.fire_focus_event(FocusEventType::Blur, node.upcast(), None, can_gc);\n1505:                 }\n1506:             }\n1507:         }\n1508: \n1509:         if old_focus_state != new_focus_state && !new_focus_state {\n1510:             self.fire_focus_event(FocusEventType::Blur, self.global().upcast(), None, can_gc);\n1511:         }\n1512: \n1513: self.fire_focus_event(FocusEventType::Focus, self.global().upcast(), None, can_gc);\n1518:         }\n1519: \n1520:         if old_focused_filtered != new_focused_filtered {\n1521:             if let Some(elem) = &new_focused_filtered {\n1522:                 elem.set_focus_state(true);\n1523:                 let node = elem.upcast::<Node>();\n1524:                 // FIXME: pass appropriate relatedTarget\n1525:                 self.fire_focus_event(FocusEventType::Focus, node.upcast(), None, can_gc);\n1526: \n1527:                 // Scroll operation to happen after element gets focus. This is needed to ensure that the\n1528:                 // focused element is visible. Only scroll if preventScroll was not specified.\n1529:                 if !prevent_scroll {\n1530:                     // We are following the firefox implementation where we are only scrolling to the element\n1531:                     // if the element itself it not visible.\n1532:                     let scroll_axis = ScrollAxisState {\n1533:                         position: ScrollLogicalPosition::Center,\n1534:                         requirement: ScrollRequirement::IfNotVisible,\n1535:                     };\n1536: \n1537:                     // TODO(stevennovaryo): we doesn't differentiate focus operation from script and from user\n1538:                     //                      for a scroll yet.\n1539:                     // TODO(#40474): Implement specific     scroll_axis,\n1543:                         scroll_axis,\n1544:                         None,\n1545:                         None,\n1546:                     );\n1547:                 }\n1548:             }\n1549:         }\n1550: \n1551:         if focus_initiator != FocusInitiator::Local {\n1552:             return;\n1553:         }\n1554: \n1555:         // We are the initiator of the focus operation, so we must broadcast\n1556:         // the change we intend to make.\n1557:         match (old_focus_state, new_focus_state) {\n1558:             (_, true) => {\n1559:                 // Advertise the change in the focus chain.\n1560:                 // <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#focus-chain>\n1561:                 // <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#focusing-steps>\n1562:                 //\n1563:                 // If the top-level BC doesn't have system focus, this won't\n1564:                 // have an immediate effect, but it will when we gain system\n1565:                 // focus again. Therefore we still have to send `ScriptMsg::\n1566:                 // Focus`.\n1567:                 //\n1568:                 // When a container with a non-null nested browsing context is\n1569:                 // focused, its active document becomes the focused area of the\n1570:                 // top-level browsing context instead. Therefore we need to let\n1571:                 // the constellation know if such a container is focused.\n1572:                 //\n1573:                 // > The focusing steps for an object `new focus target` [...]\n1574:                 // >\n1575:                 // >  3. If `new focus target` is a browsing context container\n1576:                 // >     with non-null nested browsing context, then set\n1577:                 // >     `new focus target` to the nested browsing context's\n1578:                 // >     active document.\n1579:                 let child_browsing_context_id = new_focused\n1580:                     .as_ref()\n1581:                     .and_then(|elem| elem.downcast::<HTMLIFrameElement>())\n1582:                     .and_then(|iframe| iframe.browsing_context_id());\n1583: \n1584:                 let sequence = self.increment_fetch_focus_sequence();\n1585: \n1586:                 debug!(\n1587:                     \"Advertising the focus request to the constellation \\\n1588:                         with sequence number {} and child BC ID {}\",\n1589:                     sequence,\n1590:                     child_browsing_context_id\n1591:                         .as_ref()\n1592:                         .map(|id| id as &dyn std::fmt::Display)\n1593:                         .unwrap_or(&\"(none)\"),\n1594:                 );\n1595: \n1596:                 self.window()\n1597:                     .send_to_constellation(ScriptToConstellationMessage::Focus(\n1598:                         child_browsing_context_id,\n1599:                         sequence,\n1600:                     ));\n1601:             },\n1602:             (false, false) => {\n1603:                 // Our `Document` doesn't have focus, and we intend to keep it\n1604:                 // this way.\n1605:             },\n1606:             (true, false) => {\n1607:                 unreachable!(\n1608:                     \"Can't lose the document's focus without specifying \\\n1609:                     another one to focus\"\n1610:                 );\n1611:             },\n1612:         }\n1613:     }\n1614: \n1615:     /// Handles any updates when the document's title has changed.\n1616:     pub(crate) fn The difference\n1636:     /// here is that when the title isn't specified `None` is returned.\n1637:     fn title(&self) -> Option<DOMString> {\n1638:         let title = DOMString::from(str_join(value.str().split_html_space_characters(), \" \"))\n1659:         })\n1660:     }\n1661: \n1662:     /// Sends this document's title to the constellation.\n1663:             self.send_to_embedder(EmbedderMsg::ChangePageTitle(self.webview_id(), title));\n1668:         }\n1669:     }\n1670: \n1671:     pub(crate) fn send_to_embedder(&self, msg: EmbedderMsg) {\n1672:         let window = self.window();\n1673:         window.send_to_embedder(msg);\n1674:     }\n1675: \n1676:     pub(crate) fn dirty_all_nodes(&self) {\n1677:         let root = match        .traverse_preorder(ShadowIncluding::Yes)\n1684:         {\n1685:             node.dirty(NodeDamage::Other)\n1686:         }\n1687:     }\n1688: \n1689:     /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#document-run-the-scroll-steps>\n1690:     pub(crate) fn run_the_scroll_steps(&self, can_gc: CanGc) {\n1691:         // Step 1.\n1692:         // > Run the steps to dispatch pending scrollsnapchanging events for doc.\n1693:         // TODO(#7673): Implement scroll snapping\n1694: \n1695:         // Step 2\n1696:         // > For each item target in doc’s pending scroll event targets, in the order they\n1697:         // > were added to the list, run these substeps:\n1698:         // Step 3.\n1699:         // > Empty doc’s pending scroll event targets.\n1700:         // Since the scroll event callback could trigger another scroll event, we are taking all of the\n1701:         // current scroll event to avoid borrow checking error.\n1702:         rooted_vec!(let notify_list <- self.pending_scroll_event_targets.take().into_iter());\n1703:         for target in notify_list.iter() {\n1704:             if target.downcast::<Document>().is_some() {\n1705:                 // Step 2.1\n1706:                 // > If target is a Document, fire an event named scroll that bubbles at target.\n1707:                 target.fire_bubbling_event(Atom::from(\"scroll\"), can_gc);\n1708:             } else if target.downcast::<Element>().is_some() {\n1709:                 // Step 2.2\n1710:                 // > 4.\n1716:         // > Run the steps to dispatch pending scrollsnapchange events for doc.\n1717:         // TODO(#7673): Implement scroll snapping\n1718:     }\n1719: \n1720:     /// Whenever a viewport gets scrolled (whether in response to user interaction or by an\n1721:     /// API), the user agent must run these steps:\n1722:     /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#scrolling-events>\n1723:     pub(crate) fn handle_viewport_scroll_event(&self) {\n1724:         // Step 2.\n1725:         // > If doc is a snap container, run the a snap container, run the steps to update scrollsnapchanging\n1755:         // > targets for the element with the element’s eventual snap target in the block\n1756:         // > axis as newBlockTarget we've received.\n    ///\n    /// A rAF request is considered spurious if nothing was actually reflowed.\n    spurious_animation_frames: Cell<u8>,\n\n    /// Track the total number of elements in this also: <https://github.com/servo/servo/issues/10110>\n    dom_count: Cell<u32>,\n    /// Entry node for fullscreen.\n    fullscreen_element: MutNullableDom<Element>,\n    /// Map from ID to set of form DomRefCell<HashMapTracedValues<Atom, HashSet<Dom<Element>>, FxBuildHasher>>,\n    #[no_trace]\n    interactive_time: DomRefCell<ProgressiveWebMetrics>,\n    #[no_trace]\n    tti_window: DomRefCell<InteractiveWindow>,\n    /// RAII canceller for Fetch\n    canceller: FetchCanceller,\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#throw-on-dynamic-markup-insertion-counter>\n    throw_on_dynamic_markup_insertion_counter: Cell<u64>,\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#page-showing>\n    page_showing: Cell<bool>,\n    /// Whether the document is salvageable.\n fired_unload: Cell<bool>,\n    /// List of responsive images\n    responsive_images: DomRefCell<Vec<Dom<HTMLImageElement>>>,\n    /// Number of redirects for the document load\n    redirect_count: blocker is removed.\n    #[ignore_malloc_size_of = \"Measuring trait objects is hard\"]\n    delayed_tasks: DomRefCell<Vec<Box<dyn NonSendTaskBox>>>,\n    ///    /// We need to keep this list to allow the media controls to\n    /// access the \"privileged\" document.servoGetMediaControls(id) API,\n    /// where `id` needs to match any of the registered ShadowRoots\n    /// hosting the media controls UI.\n    media_controls: DomRefCell<HashMap<String, Dom<ShadowRoot>>>,\n    /// A set of dirty HTML canvas elements that need their WebRender images updated the\n    /// next time the rendering is updated.\n    dirty_canvases: DomRefCell<Vec<Dom<HTMLCanvasElement>>>,\n    /// Whether or not animated images need to have their contents updated.\n    has_pending_animated_image_update: Cell<bool>,\n    /// <https://w3c.github.io/slection-api/#dfn-selection>\n    selection: MutNullableDom<Selection>,\n    /// A timeline for animations which is used for synchronizing animations.\n    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/web-animations/#timeline>\n    animation_timeline: DomRefCell<AnimationTimeline>,\n    /// Animations for this Document\n    animations: Animations,\n    /// Image Animation Manager for this Document\n    image_animation_manager: DomRefCell<ImageAnimationManager>,\n    /// The nearest inclusive ancestors to all the nodes that require a restyle.\n    dirty_root: MutNullableDom<Element>,\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#will-declaratively-refresh>\n    declarative_refresh: DomRefCell<Option<DeclarativeRefresh>>,\n    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/resize-observer/#dom-document-resizeobservers-slot>\n    ///\n    /// Note: we are storing, but never removing, resize observers.\n    /// The lifetime of resize observers is specified at\n    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/resize-observer/#lifetime>.\n    /// But implementing it comes with known problems:\n    /// - <https://bugzilla.mozilla.org/show_bug.cgi?id=1596992>\n    /// - <https://github.com/w3c/csswg-drafts/issues/4518>\n    resize_observers: DomRefCell<Vec<Dom<ResizeObserver>>>,\n    /// The set of all fonts loaded by this document.\n    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-font-loading/#font-face-source>\n    fonts: MutNullableDom<FontFaceSet>,\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#visibility-state>\n    visibility_state: Cell<DocumentVisibilityState>,\n    /// <https://www.iana.org/assignments/http-status-codes/http-status-codes.xhtml>\n    status_code: Option<u16>,\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#is-initial-about:blank>\n    is_initial_about_blank: Cell<bool>,\n    /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#document-allow-declarative-shadow-roots>\n    allow_declarative_shadow_roots: Cell<bool>,\n    /// <https://w3c.github.io/webappsec-upgrade-insecure-requests/#insecure-requests-policy>\n    #[no_trace]\n    inherited_insecure_requests_policy: Cell<Option<InsecureRequestsPolicy>>,\n    //// <https://w3c.github.io/webappsec-mixed-content/#categorize-settings-object>\n    has_trustworthy_ancestor_origin: Cell<bool>,\n    /// processed by this document in\n    /// the update intersection observation steps.\n    /// <https://w3c.github.io/IntersectionObserver/#run-the-update-intersection-observations-steps>\n    /// > Let observer list be a list of all IntersectionObservers whose root is in the DOM tree of document.\n    /// > For the top-level browsing context, this includes implicit root observers.\n    ///\n    /// Details of which document that should process an observers is discussed further at\n    /// <https://github.com/w3c/IntersectionObserver/issues/525>.\n    ///\n    /// The lifetime of an intersection observer is specified at\n    /// <https://github.com/w3c/IntersectionObserver/issues/525>.\n    intersection_observers: DomRefCell<Vec<Dom<IntersectionObserver>>>,\n    /// The node that is currently highlighted by the devtools\n    highlighted_dom_node: MutNullableDom<Node>,\n    /// The constructed stylesheet that is adopted by this [Document].\n    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-documentorshadowroot-adoptedstylesheets>\n    adopted_stylesheets: DomRefCell<Vec<Dom<CSSStyleSheet>>>,\n    /// Cached frozen array of <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#document-pending-scroll-event-targets>\n    pending_scroll_event_targets: DomRefCell<Vec<Dom<EventTarget>>>,\n    /// Other reasons that a rendering update might be required for this [`Document`].\n    rendering_update_reasons: Cell<RenderingUpdateReason>,\n    /// Whether or not this [`Document`] is waiting on canvas image updates. If it is\n    /// waiting it will not do any new layout until the canvas images are up-to-date in\n    /// the renderer.\n    waiting_on_canvas_image_updates: Cell<bool>,\n    /// The current rendering epoch, which is used to track updates in the renderer.\n    ///\n    ///   - Every display list update also advances the Epoch, so that the renderer knows\n    ///     when a particular display list is ready in order to take a screenshot.\n    ///   - Canvas image updates happen asynchronously and are tagged with this Epoch. Until\n    ///     those asynchronous updates are complete, the `Document` will not perform any\n    ///     more rendering updates.\n    #[no_trace]\n    current_rendering_epoch: Cell<Epoch>,\n    /// The global custom element reaction stack for this script thread.\n    #[conditional_malloc_size_of]\n    custom_element_reaction_stack: Rc<CustomElementReactionStack>,\n    #[no_trace]\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#active-sandboxing-flag-set>,\n    active_sandboxing_flag_set: Cell<SandboxingFlagSet>,\n    #[no_trace]\n    /// The [`SandboxingFlagSet`] use to create the browsing context for this [`Document`].\n    /// These are cached here as they cannot always be retrieved readily if the owner of\n    /// browsing context (either `<iframe>` or popup) might be in a different `ScriptThread`.\n    ///\n    /// See\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#determining-the-creation-sandboxing-flags>.\n    creation_sandboxing_flag_set: Cell<SandboxingFlagSet>,\n    /// The cached favicon for that document.\n    #[no_trace]\n    #[ignore_malloc_size_of = \"TODO: unimplemented on Image\"]\n    favicon: RefCell<Option<Image>>,\n\n    /// All websockets created that are associated with this document.\n    websockets: DOMTracker<WebSocket>,\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#details-name-group>\n    details_name_groups: DomRefCell<Option<DetailsNameGroups>>,\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#registerprotocolhandler()-automation-mode>\n    #[no_trace]\n    protocol_handler_automation_mode: RefCell<CustomHandlersAutomationMode>,\n\n    /// Reflect the value of that preferences to prevent paying the cost of a RwLock access.\n    layout_animations_test_enabled: bool,\n}\n\nimpl Document {\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#unloading-document-cleanup-steps>\n    fn unloading_cleanup_steps(&self) {\n        // Step 1. Let window be document's relevant global object.\n        // Step 2. For each WebSocket object webSocket whose relevant global object is window, make disappear webSocket.\n        if self.close_outstanding_websockets() {\n            // If this affected any WebSocket objects, then make document unsalvageable given document and \"websocket\".\n            self.salvageable.set(false);\n        }\n\n        // Step 3. For each WebTransport object transport whose relevant global object is window, run the context cleanup steps given transport.\n        // TODO\n\n        // Step 4. If document's salvageable state is false, then:\n        if !self.salvageable.get() {\n            let global_scope = self.window.as_global_scope();\n\n            // Step 4.1. For each EventSource object eventSource whose relevant global object is equal to window, forcibly close eventSource.\n            global_scope.close_event_sources();\n\n            // Step 4.2. Clear window's map of active timers.\n            // TODO\n\n            // Ensure the constellation discards all bfcache information for this document.\n            let msg = ScriptToConstellationMessage::DiscardDocument;\n            let _ = global_scope.script_to_constellation_chan().send(msg);\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn track_websocket(&self, websocket: &WebSocket) {\n        self.websockets.track(websocket);\n    }\n\n    fn close_outstanding_websockets(&self) -> bool {\n        let mut closed_any_websocket = false;\n        self.websockets.for_each(|websocket: DomRoot<WebSocket>| {\n            if websocket.make_disappear() {\n                closed_any_websocket = true;\n            }\n        });\n        closed_any_websocket\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn note_node_with_dirty_descendants(&self, node: &Node) {\n        debug_assert!(*node.owner_doc() == *self);\n        if !node.is_connected() {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        let parent = match node.parent_in_flat_tree() {\n            Some(parent) => parent,\n            None => {\n                // There is no parent so this is the Document node, so we\n                // behave as if we were called with the document element.\n                let document_element = match self.GetDocumentElement() {\n                    Some(element) => element,\n                    None => return,\n                };\n                if let Some(dirty_root) = self.dirty_root.get() {\n                    // There was an existing dirty root so we mark its\n                    // ancestors as dirty until the document element.\n                    for ancestor in dirty_root\n                        .upcast::<Node>()\n                        .inclusive_ancestors_in_flat_tree()\n                    {\n                        if ancestor.is::<Element>() {\n                            ancestor.set_flag(NodeFlags::HAS_DIRTY_DESCENDANTS, true);\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                self.dirty_root.set(Some(&document_element));\n                return;\n            },\n        };\n\n        if parent.is::<Element>() {\n            if !parent.is_styled() {\n                return;\n            }\n\n            if parent.is_display_none() {\n                return;\n            }\n        }\n\n        let element_parent: DomRoot<Element>;\n        let element = match node.downcast::<Element>() {\n            Some(element) => element,\n            None => {\n                // Current node is not an element, it's probably a text node,\n                // we try to get its element parent.\n                match DomRoot::downcast::<Element>(parent) {\n                    Some(parent) => {\n                        element_parent = parent;\n                        &element_parent\n                    },\n                    None => {\n                        // Parent is not an element so it must be a document,\n                        // and this is not an element either, so there is\n                        // nothing to do.\n                        return;\n                    },\n                }\n            },\n        };\n\n        let dirty_root = match self.dirty_root.get() {\n            Some(root) if root.is_connected() => root,\n            _ => {\n                element\n                    .upcast::<Node>()\n                    .set_flag(NodeFlags::HAS_DIRTY_DESCENDANTS, true);\n                self.dirty_root.set(Some(element));\n                return;\n            },\n        };\n\n        for ancestor in element.upcast::<Node>().inclusive_ancestors_in_flat_tree() {\n            if ancestor.get_flag(NodeFlags::HAS_DIRTY_DESCENDANTS) {\n                return;\n            }\n\n            if ancestor.is::<Element>() {\n                ancestor.set_flag(NodeFlags::HAS_DIRTY_DESCENDANTS, true);\n            }\n        }\n\n        let new_dirty_root = element\n            .upcast::<Node>()\n            .common_ancestor_in_flat_tree(dirty_root.upcast())\n            .expect(\"Couldn't find common ancestor\");\n\n        let mut has_dirty_descendants = true;\n        for ancestor in dirty_root\n            .upcast::<Node>()\n            .inclusive_ancestors_in_flat_tree()\n        {\n            ancestor.set_flag(NodeFlags::HAS_DIRTY_DESCENDANTS, has_dirty_descendants);\n            has_dirty_descendants &= *ancestor != *new_dirty_root;\n        }\n\n        self.dirty_root\n            .set(Some(new_dirty_root.downcast::<Element>().unwrap()));\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn take_dirty_root(&self) -> Option<DomRoot<Element>> {\n        self.dirty_root.take()\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub(crate) fn loader(&self) -> Ref<'_, DocumentLoader> {\n        self.loader.borrow()\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub(crate) fn loader_mut(&self) -> RefMut<'_, DocumentLoader> {\n        self.loader.borrow_mut()\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub(crate) fn has_browsing_context(&self) -> bool {\n        self.has_browsing_context\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#concept-document-bc>\n    #[inline]\n    pub(crate) fn browsing_context(&self) -> Option<DomRoot<WindowProxy>> {\n        if self.has_browsing_context {\n            self.window.undiscarded_window_proxy()\n        } else {\n            None\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn webview_id(&self) -> WebViewId {\n        self.window.webview_id()\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub(crate) fn window(&self) -> &Window {\n        &self.window\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub(crate) fn is_html_document(&self) -> bool {\n        self.is_html_document\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn is_xhtml_document(&self) -> bool {\n        self.content_type.matches(APPLICATION, \"xhtml+xml\")\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn set_https_state(&self, https_state: HttpsState) {\n        self.https_state.set(https_state);\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn is_fully_active(&self) -> bool {\n        self.activity.get() == DocumentActivity::FullyActive\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn is_active(&self) -> bool {\n        self.activity.get() != DocumentActivity::Inactive\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    pub(crate) fn current_rendering_epoch(&self) -> Epoch {\n        self.current_rendering_epoch.get()\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn set_activity(&self, activity: DocumentActivity, can_gc: CanGc) {\n        // This function should only be called on documents with a browsing context\n        assert!(self.has_browsing_context);\n        if activity == self.activity.get() {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Set the document's activity level, reflow if necessary, and suspend or resume timers.\n        self.activity.set(activity);\n        let media = ServoMedia::get();\n        let pipeline_id = self.window().pipeline_id();\n        let client_context_id =\n            ClientContextId::build(pipeline_id.namespace_id.0, pipeline_id.index.0.get());\n\n        if activity != DocumentActivity::FullyActive {\n            self.window().suspend(can_gc);\n            media.suspend(&client_context_id);\n            return;\n        }\n\n        self.title_changed();\n        self.notify_embedder_favicon();\n        self.dirty_all_nodes();\n        self.window().resume(can_gc);\n        media.resume(&client_context_id);\n\n        if self.ready_state.get() != DocumentReadyState::Complete {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // This step used to be Step 4.6 in html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#history-traversal\n        // But it's now Step 4 in https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#reactivate-a-document\n        // TODO: See #32687 for more information.\n        let document = Trusted::new(self);\n        self.owner_global()\n            .task_manager()\n            .dom_manipulation_task_source()\n            .queue(task!(fire_pageshow_event: move || {\n                let document = document.root();\n                let window = document.window();\n                // Step 4.6.1\n                if document.page_showing.get() {\n                    return;\n                }\n                // Step 4.6.2 Set document's page showing flag to true.\n                document.page_showing.set(true);\n                // Step 4.6.3 Update the visibility state of document to \"visible\".\n                document.update_visibility_state(DocumentVisibilityState::Visible, CanGc::note());\n                // Step 4.6.4 Fire a page transition event named pageshow at document's relevant\n                // global object with true.\n                let event = PageTransitionEvent::new(\n                    window,\n                    atom!(\"pageshow\"),\n                    false, // bubbles\n                    false, // cancelable\n                    true, // persisted\n                    CanGc::note(),\n                );\n                let event = event.upcast::<Event>();\n                event.set_trusted(true);\n                window.dispatch_event_with_target_override(event, CanGc::note());\n            }))\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn origin(&self) -> &MutableOrigin {\n        &self.origin\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn set_protocol_handler_automation_mode(&self, mode: CustomHandlersAutomationMode) {\n        *self.protocol_handler_automation_mode.borrow_mut() = mode;\n    }\n\n    /// <https://dom.spec.whatwg.org/#concept-document-url>\n    pub(crate) fn url(&self) -> ServoUrl {\n        self.url.borrow().clone()\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn set_url(&self, url: ServoUrl) {\n        *self.url.borrow_mut() = url;\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn about_base_url(&self) -> Option<ServoUrl> {\n        self.about_base_url.borrow().clone()\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn set_about_base_url(&self, about_base_url: Option<ServoUrl>) {\n        *self.about_base_url.borrow_mut() = about_base_url;\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#fallback-base-url>\n    pub(crate) fn fallback_base_url(&self) -> ServoUrl {\n        let document_url = self.url();\n        // Step 1: If document is an iframe srcdoc document:\n        if document_url.as_str() == \"about:srcdoc\" {\n            // Step 1.1: Assert: document's about base URL is non-null.\n            // Step 1.2: Return document's about base URL.\n            return self\n                .about_base_url()\n                .expect(\"about:srcdoc page should always have an about base URL\");\n        }\n\n        // Step 2: If document's URL matches about:blank and document's about base URL is\n        // non-null, then return document's about base URL.\n        if document_url.matches_about_blank() {\n            if let Some(about_base_url) = self.about_base_url() {\n                return about_base_url;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Step 3: Return document's URL.\n        document_url\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#document-base-url>\n    pub(crate) fn base_url(&self) -> ServoUrl {\n        match self.base_element() {\n            // Step 1.\n            None => self.fallback_base_url(),\n            // Step 2.\n            Some(base) => base.frozen_base_url(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn add_restyle_reason(&self, reason: RestyleReason) {\n        self.needs_restyle.set(self.needs_restyle.get() | reason)\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn clear_restyle_reasons(&self) {\n        self.needs_restyle.set(RestyleReason::empty());\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn restyle_reason(&self) -> RestyleReason {\n        let mut condition = self.needs_restyle.get();\n        if self.stylesheets.borrow().has_changed() {\n            condition.insert(RestyleReason::StylesheetsChanged);\n        }\n\n        // FIXME: This should check the dirty bit on the document,\n        // not the document element. Needs some layout changes to make\n        // that workable.\n        if let Some(root) = self.GetDocumentElement() {\n            if root.upcast::<Node>().has_dirty_descendants() {\n                condition.insert(RestyleReason::DOMChanged);\n            }\n        }\n\n        if !self.pending_restyles.borrow().is_empty() {\n            condition.insert(RestyleReason::PendingRestyles);\n        }\n\n        condition\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the first `base` element in the DOM that has an `href` attribute.\n    pub(crate) fn base_element(&self) -> Option<DomRoot<HTMLBaseElement>> {\n        self.base_element.get()\n    }\n\n    /// Refresh the cached first base element in the DOM.\n    /// <https://github.com/w3c/web-platform-tests/issues/2122>\n    pub(crate) fn refresh_base_element(&self) {\n        if let Some(base_element) = self.base_element.get() {\n            base_element.clear_frozen_base_url();\n        }\n        let new_base_element = self\n            .upcast::<Node>()\n            .traverse_preorder(ShadowIncluding::No)\n            .filter_map(DomRoot::downcast::<HTMLBaseElement>)\n            .find(|element| {\n                element\n                    .upcast::<Element>()\n                    .has_attribute(&local_name!(\"href\"))\n            });\n        if let Some(ref new_base_element) = new_base_element {\n            new_base_element.set_frozen_base_url();\n        }\n        self.base_element.set(new_base_element.as_deref());\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn dom_count(&self) -> u32 {\n        self.dom_count.get()\n    }\n\n    /// This is called by `bind_to_tree` when a node is added to the DOM.\n    /// The internal count is used by layout to determine whether to be sequential or parallel.\n    /// (it's sequential for small DOMs)\n    pub(crate) fn increment_dom_count(&self) {\n        self.dom_count.set(self.dom_count.get() + 1);\n    }\n\n    /// This is called by `unbind_from_tree` when a node is removed from the DOM.\n    pub(crate) fn decrement_dom_count(&self) {\n        self.dom_count.set(self.dom_count.get() - 1);\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn quirks_mode(&self) -> QuirksMode {\n        self.quirks_mode.get()\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn set_quirks_mode(&self, new_mode: QuirksMode) {\n        let old_mode = self.quirks_mode.replace(new_mode);\n\n        if old_mode != new_mode {\n            self.window.layout_mut().set_quirks_mode(new_mode);\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn encoding(&self) -> &'static Encoding {\n        self.encoding.get()\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn set_encoding(&self, encoding: &'static Encoding) {\n        self.encoding.set(encoding);\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn content_and_heritage_changed(&self, node: &Node) {\n        if node.is_connected() {\n            node.note_dirty_descendants();\n        }\n\n        // FIXME(emilio): This is very inefficient, ideally the flag above would\n        // be enough and incremental layout could figure out from there.\n        node.dirty(NodeDamage::ContentOrHeritage);\n    }\n\n    /// Remove any existing association between the provided id and any elements in this document.\n    pub(crate) fn unregister_element_id(&self, to_unregister: &Element, id: Atom, can_gc: CanGc) {\n        self.document_or_shadow_root\n            .unregister_named_element(&self.id_map, to_unregister, &id);\n        self.reset_form_owner_for_listeners(&id, can_gc);\n    }\n\n    /// Associate an element present in this document with the provided id.\n    pub(crate) fn register_element_id(&self, element: &Element, id: Atom, can_gc: CanGc) {\n        let root = self.GetDocumentElement().expect(\n            \"The element is in the document, so there must be a document \\\n             element.\",\n        );\n        self.document_or_shadow_root.register_named_element(\n            &self.id_map,\n            element,\n            &id,\n            DomRoot::from_ref(root.upcast::<Node>()),\n        );\n        self.reset_form_owner_for_listeners(&id, can_gc);\n    }\n\n    /// Remove any existing association between the provided name and any elements in this document.\n    pub(crate) fn unregister_element_name(&self, to_unregister: &Element, name: Atom) {\n        self.document_or_shadow_root\n            .unregister_named_element(&self.name_map, to_unregister, &name);\n    }\n\n    /// Associate an element present in this document with the provided name.\n    pub(crate) fn register_element_name(&self, element: &Element, name: Atom) {\n        let root = self.GetDocumentElement().expect(\n            \"The element is in the document, so there must be a document \\\n             element.\",\n        );\n        self.document_or_shadow_root.register_named_element(\n            &self.name_map,\n            element,\n            &name,\n            DomRoot::from_ref(root.upcast::<Node>()),\n        );\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn register_form_id_listener<T: ?Sized + FormControl>(\n        &self,\n        id: DOMString,\n        listener: &T,\n    ) {\n        let mut map = self.form_id_listener_map.borrow_mut();\n        let listener = listener.to_element();\n        let set = map.entry(Atom::from(id)).or_default();\n        set.insert(Dom::from_ref(listener));\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn unregister_form_id_listener<T: ?Sized + FormControl>(\n        &self,\n        id: DOMString,\n        listener: &T,\n    ) {\n        let mut map = self.form_id_listener_map.borrow_mut();\n        if let Occupied(mut entry) = map.entry(Atom::from(id)) {\n            entry\n                .get_mut()\n                .remove(&Dom::from_ref(listener.to_element()));\n            if entry.get().is_empty() {\n                entry.remove();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#find-a-potential-indicated-element>\n    fn find_a_potential_indicated_element(&self, fragment: &str) -> Option<DomRoot<Element>> {\n        // Step 1. If there is an element in the document tree whose root is\n        // document and that has an ID equal to fragment, then return the first such element in tree order.\n        // Step 3. Return null.\n        self.get_element_by_id(&Atom::from(fragment))\n            // Step 2. If there is an a element in the document tree whose root is\n            // document that has a name attribute whose value is equal to fragment,\n            // then return the first such element in tree order.\n            .or_else(|| self.get_anchor_by_name(fragment))\n    }\n\n    /// Attempt to find a named element in this page's document.\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#the-indicated-part-of-the-document>\n    fn select_indicated_part(&self, fragment: &str) -> Option<DomRoot<Node>> {\n        // Step 1. If document's URL does not equal url with exclude fragments set to true, then return null.\n        //\n        // Already handled by calling function\n\n        // Step 2. Let fragment be url's fragment.\n        //\n        // Already handled by calling function\n\n        // Step 3. If fragment is the empty string, then return the special value top of the document.\n        if fragment.is_empty() {\n            return Some(DomRoot::from_ref(self.upcast()));\n        }\n        // Step 4. Let potentialIndicatedElement be the result of finding a potential indicated element given document and fragment.\n        if let Some(potential_indicated_element) = self.find_a_potential_indicated_element(fragment)\n        {\n            // Step 5. If potentialIndicatedElement is not null, then return potentialIndicatedElement.\n            return Some(DomRoot::upcast(potential_indicated_element));\n        }\n        // Step 6. Let fragmentBytes be the result of percent-decoding fragment.\n        let fragment_bytes = percent_decode(fragment.as_bytes());\n        // Step 7. Let decodedFragment be the result of running UTF-8 decode without BOM on fragmentBytes.\n        let Ok(decoded_fragment) = fragment_bytes.decode_utf8() else {\n            return None;\n        };\n        // Step 8. Set potentialIndicatedElement to the result of finding a potential indicated element given document and decodedFragment.\n        if let Some(potential_indicated_element) =\n            self.find_a_potential_indicated_element(&decoded_fragment)\n        {\n            // Step 9. If potentialIndicatedElement is not null, then return potentialIndicatedElement.\n            return Some(DomRoot::upcast(potential_indicated_element));\n        }\n        // Step 10. If decodedFragment is an ASCII case-insensitive match for the string top, then return the top of the document.\n        if decoded_fragment.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"top\") {\n            return Some(DomRoot::from_ref(self.upcast()));\n        }\n        // Step 11. Return null.\n        None\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#scroll-to-the-fragment-identifier>\n    pub(crate) fn scroll_to_the_fragment(&self, fragment: &str) {\n        // Step 1. If document's indicated part is null, then set document's target element to null.\n        //\n        // > For an HTML document document, its indicated part is the result of\n        // > selecting the indicated part given document and document's URL.\n        let Some(indicated_part) = self.select_indicated_part(fragment) else {\n            self.set_target_element(None);\n            return;\n        };\n        // Step 2. Otherwise, if document's indicated part is top of the document, then:\n        if *indicated_part == *self.upcast() {\n            // Step 2.1. Set document's target element to null.\n            self.set_target_element(None);\n            // Step 2.2. Scroll to the beginning of the document for document. [CSSOMVIEW]\n            //\n            // FIXME(stshine): this should be the origin of the stacking context space,\n            // which may differ under the influence of writing mode.\n            self.window.scroll(0.0, 0.0, ScrollBehavior::Instant);\n            // Step 2.3. Return.\n            return;\n        }\n        // Step 3. Otherwise:\n        // Step 3.2. Let target be document's indicated part.\n        let Some(target) = indicated_part.downcast::<Element>() else {\n            // Step 3.1. Assert: document's indicated part is an element.\n            unreachable!(\"Indicated part should always be an element\");\n        };\n        // Step 3.3. Set document's target element to target.\n        self.set_target_element(Some(target));\n        // Step 3.4. Run the ancestor revealing algorithm on target.\n        // TODO\n        // Step 3.5. Scroll target into view, with behavior set to \"auto\", block set to \"start\", and inline set to \"nearest\". [CSSOMVIEW]\n        target.scroll_into_view_with_options(\n            ScrollBehavior::Auto,\n            ScrollAxisState::new_always_scroll_position(ScrollLogicalPosition::Start),\n            ScrollAxisState::new_always_scroll_position(ScrollLogicalPosition::Nearest),\n            None,\n            None,\n        );\n        // Step 3.6. Run the focusing steps for target, with the Document's viewport as the fallback target.\n        // TODO\n        // Step 3.7. Move the sequential focus navigation starting point to target.\n        // TODO\n    }\n\n    fn get_anchor_by_name(&self, name: &str) -> Option<DomRoot<Element>> {\n        let name = Atom::from(name);\n        self.name_map.borrow().get(&name).and_then(|elements| {\n            elements\n                .iter()\n                .find(|e| e.is::<HTMLAnchorElement>())\n                .map(|e| DomRoot::from_ref(&**e))\n        })\n    }\n\n    // https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#current-document-readiness\n    pub(crate) fn set_ready_state(&self, state: DocumentReadyState, can_gc: CanGc) {\n        match state {\n            DocumentReadyState::Loading => {\n                if self.window().is_top_level() {\n                    self.send_to_embedder(EmbedderMsg::NotifyLoadStatusChanged(\n                        self.webview_id(),\n                        LoadStatus::Started,\n                    ));\n                    self.send_to_embedder(EmbedderMsg::Status(self.webview_id(), None));\n                }\n            },\n            DocumentReadyState::Complete => {\n                if self.window().is_top_level() {\n                    self.send_to_embedder(EmbedderMsg::NotifyLoadStatusChanged(\n                        self.webview_id(),\n                        LoadStatus::Complete,\n                    ));\n                }\n                update_with_current_instant(&self.dom_complete);\n            },\n            DocumentReadyState::Interactive => update_with_current_instant(&self.dom_interactive),\n        };\n\n        self.ready_state.set(state);\n\n        self.upcast::<EventTarget>()\n            .fire_event(atom!(\"readystatechange\"), can_gc);\n    }\n\n    /// Return whether scripting is enabled or not\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#concept-n-script>\n    pub(crate) fn scripting_enabled(&self) -> bool {\n        // Scripting is enabled for a node node if node's node document's browsing context is non-null,\n        // and scripting is enabled for node's relevant settings object.\n        self.has_browsing_context() &&\n        // Either settings's global object is not a Window object,\n        // or settings's global object's associated Document's active sandboxing flag\n        // set does not have its sandboxed scripts browsing context flag set.\n            !self.has_active_sandboxing_flag(\n                SandboxingFlagSet::SANDBOXED_SCRIPTS_BROWSING_CONTEXT_FLAG,\n            )\n    }\n\n    /// Return the element that currently has focus.\n    // https://w3c.github.io/uievents/#events-focusevent-doc-focus\n    pub(crate) fn get_focused_element(&self) -> Option<DomRoot<Element>> {\n        self.focused.get()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the last sequence number sent to the constellation.\n    ///\n    /// Received focus-related messages with sequence numbers less than the one\n    /// returned by this method must be discarded.\n    pub fn get_focus_sequence(&self) -> FocusSequenceNumber {\n        self.focus_sequence.get()\n    }\n\n    /// Generate the next sequence number for focus-related messages.\n    fn increment_fetch_focus_sequence(&self) -> FocusSequenceNumber {\n        self.focus_sequence.set(FocusSequenceNumber(\n            self.focus_sequence\n                .get()\n                .0\n                .checked_add(1)\n                .expect(\"too many focus messages have been sent\"),\n        ));\n        self.focus_sequence.get()\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn has_focus_transaction(&self) -> bool {\n        self.focus_transaction.borrow().is_some()\n    }\n\n    /// Initiate a new round of checking for elements requesting focus. The last element to call\n    /// `request_focus` before `commit_focus_transaction` is called will receive focus.\n    pub(crate) fn begin_focus_transaction(&self) {\n        // Initialize it with the current state\n        *self.focus_transaction.borrow_mut() = Some(FocusTransaction {\n            element: self.focused.get().as_deref().map(Dom::from_ref),\n            has_focus: self.has_focus.get(),\n            focus_options: FocusOptions {\n                preventScroll: true,\n            },\n        });\n    }\n\n    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#focus-fixup-rule>\n    pub(crate) fn perform_focus_fixup_rule(&self, not_focusable: &Element, can_gc: CanGc) {\n        // Return if `not_focusable` is not the designated focused area of the\n        // `Document`.\n        if Some(not_focusable) != self.focused.get().as_deref() {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        let implicit_transaction = self.focus_transaction.borrow().is_none();\n\n        if implicit_transaction {\n            self.begin_focus_transaction();\n        }\n\n        // Designate the viewport as the new focused area of the `Document`, but\n        // do not run the focusing steps.\n        {\n            let mut focus_transaction = self.focus_transaction.borrow_mut();\n            focus_transaction.as_mut().unwrap().element = None;\n        }\n\n        if implicit_transaction {\n            self.commit_focus_transaction(FocusInitiator::Local, can_gc);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Request that the given element receive focus with default options.\n    /// See [`Self::request_focus_with_options`] for the details.\n    pub(crate) fn request_focus(\n        &self,\n        elem: Option<&Element>,\n        focus_initiator: FocusInitiator,\n        can_gc: CanGc,\n    ) {\n        self.request_focus_with_options(\n            elem,\n            focus_initiator,\n            FocusOptions {\n                preventScroll: true,\n            },\n            can_gc,\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// Request that the given element receive focus once the current\n    /// transaction is complete. `None` specifies to focus the document.\n    ///\n    /// If there's no ongoing transaction, this method automatically starts and\n    /// commits an implicit transaction.\n    pub(crate) fn request_focus_with_options(\n        &self,\n        elem: Option<&Element>,\n        focus_initiator: FocusInitiator,\n        focus_options: FocusOptions,\n        can_gc: CanGc,\n    ) {\n        // If an element is specified, and it's non-focusable, ignore the\n        // request.\n        if elem.is_some_and(|e| !e.is_focusable_area()) {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        let implicit_transaction = self.focus_transaction.borrow().is_none();\n\n        if implicit_transaction {\n            self.begin_focus_transaction();\n        }\n\n        {\n            let mut focus_transaction = self.focus_transaction.borrow_mut();\n            let focus_transaction = focus_transaction.as_mut().unwrap();\n            focus_transaction.element = elem.map(Dom::from_ref);\n            focus_transaction.has_focus = true;\n            focus_transaction.focus_options = focus_options;\n        }\n\n        if implicit_transaction {\n            self.commit_focus_transaction(focus_initiator, can_gc);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Update the local focus state accordingly after being notified that the\n    /// document's container is removed from the top-level browsing context's\n    /// focus chain (not considering system focus).\n    pub(crate) fn handle_container_unfocus(&self, can_gc: CanGc) {\n        if self.window().parent_info().is_none() {\n            warn!(\"Top-level document cannot be unfocused\");\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Since this method is called from an event loop, there mustn't be\n        // an in-progress focus transaction\n        assert!(\n            self.focus_transaction.borrow().is_none(),\n            \"there mustn't be an in-progress focus transaction at this point\"\n        );\n\n        // Start an implicit focus transaction\n        self.begin_focus_transaction();\n\n        // Update the transaction\n        {\n            let mut
